title: Alicia

jokes.text: And who wears fake heels at breakfast time?
jokes.setup: Woman complains about walking in Louboutin heels
jokes.punchline: Dee calling out the fake designer shoes at an inappropriate time
jokes.explanation: Sharp character moment establishing Dee's no-nonsense attitude and class awareness

jokes.text: Some curb-crawlin' asshole
jokes.setup: Jonah approaches them as a reporter
jokes.punchline: Dee's immediate hostile assessment of Jonah
jokes.explanation: Economical and character-specific insult with vivid imagery

jokes.text: My website is Ryantology.net and I'm a storyteller
jokes.setup: Jonah trying to establish credibility as a journalist
jokes.punchline: The pompous self-description and ridiculous website name
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of Jonah's delusional self-importance with the absurd 'Ryantology' portmanteau

jokes.text: I'm like John Steinbeck in that regard... or Denzel Washington
jokes.setup: Jonah comparing himself to great storytellers
jokes.punchline: The bizarre pairing of a novelist with an actor as equivalent examples
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Jonah's complete lack of understanding about anything, comparing a Nobel Prize-winning novelist to an actor

jokes.text: Tell me your story. Question mark.
jokes.setup: Jonah trying to ask a profound journalistic question
jokes.punchline: Literally saying 'question mark' out loud
jokes.explanation: Perfect demonstration of Jonah's incompetence - he's so bad at interviewing he reads punctuation aloud

jokes.text: I was kinda Amy's boss at the time. It got complicated. But she's basically a good kid.
jokes.setup: Explaining his relationship with Amy
jokes.punchline: The condescending tone toward someone clearly more competent
jokes.explanation: Jonah's delusional superiority complex on full display

jokes.text: Just sittin' on the co'ner
jokes.setup: Jonah being dismissed and left alone
jokes.punchline: His pathetic attempt at street slang
jokes.explanation: Jonah's awkward attempt to sound cool while being rejected

jokes.text: Hello. Do you like Power Rangers?
jokes.setup: Amy meeting a 6-year-old for the first time
jokes.punchline: Her awkward, inappropriate conversation starter
jokes.explanation: Shows Amy's complete inability to relate to children despite the child care focus

jokes.text: Do you want to look like Alice Cooper?
jokes.setup: Warning Selina about outdoor speech risks
jokes.punchline: Comparing potential appearance to shock rocker
jokes.explanation: Vivid and specific comparison that's both accurate and absurd

jokes.text: My second marriage took place in the rain. You can see my nipples in all the photos.
jokes.setup: Discussion about outdoor events and weather
jokes.punchline: Oversharing personal details about wedding photos
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina moment - inappropriate oversharing delivered with complete casualness

jokes.text: Gettysburg, Mount Sinai... the speech I made in Philadelphia three weeks ago.
jokes.setup: Listing great outdoor speeches
jokes.punchline: Including her own recent mundane speech in the pantheon
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from historic speeches to her own recent mediocre one, showing her narcissism

jokes.text: That was inside, ma'am.
jokes.setup: Selina's grand claim about her Philadelphia speech
jokes.punchline: Gary's quiet factual correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Selina's grandiosity with simple facts

jokes.text: She's got one of the 'normals' here.
jokes.setup: Describing Amy's situation with a civilian
jokes.punchline: Referring to regular people as 'normals' like they're a different species
jokes.explanation: Brilliant encapsulation of DC insiders' view of regular citizens as alien beings

jokes.text: Space is bold, but safe at the same time. There are no votes in space. There's nothing there.
jokes.setup: Kent analyzing space as a campaign issue
jokes.punchline: The literal interpretation that space contains no voters
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kent moment - reducing everything to pure political calculation in the most literal way possible

jokes.text: Alicia Keys?
jokes.setup: Being told Alicia is on the line
jokes.punchline: Immediately assuming it's the famous singer
jokes.explanation: Shows Selina's celebrity-obsessed mindset and disconnection from regular people

jokes.text: I'm in the middle of a lot of shit right now, so I'm hanging up.
jokes.setup: Speaking warmly to Alicia
jokes.punchline: Immediately reverting to crude dismissal when she thinks call is over
jokes.explanation: Classic two-faced politician moment - warm public face, crude private reality

jokes.text: Children are of no value. Forget child care.
jokes.setup: Discussion about child care as campaign issue
jokes.punchline: Kent's brutal political calculation about children's electoral worth
jokes.explanation: Shocking and perfectly in-character for Kent to reduce children to electoral math

jokes.text: Children are vital, Kenny Rogers. We're not all planning to die alone like you.
jokes.setup: Kent's dismissal of children
jokes.punchline: Selina's cutting personal attack combined with random Kenny Rogers reference
jokes.explanation: Brilliant insult that's both personal and absurd with the random Kenny Rogers comparison

jokes.text: If your girl makes president, you guys double your pay, right?
jokes.setup: Alicia trying to make conversation about the campaign
jokes.punchline: Her naive assumption about how government pay works
jokes.explanation: Shows civilian's misunderstanding of how government actually works

jokes.text: Whatever it is I'm selling, he is not buying.
jokes.setup: Kent's cold reaction to meeting her
jokes.punchline: Her accurate assessment of his complete lack of interest
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about Kent's obvious disinterest

jokes.text: Don't worry, he's like that with all the humans.
jokes.setup: Explaining Kent's coldness
jokes.punchline: Referring to people as 'humans' like Kent is another species
jokes.explanation: Perfect characterization of Kent as inhuman data analyst

jokes.text: Leon, today is a very high-pressure day, and as you know me, I don't even like high-pressure showers.
jokes.setup: Mike explaining the stress of the day
jokes.punchline: Connecting political pressure to shower pressure
jokes.explanation: Weak pun that feels forced but fits Mike's bumbling character

jokes.text: Is that what I've been smelling? I thought maybe it was your panic.
jokes.setup: Mike mentioning he doesn't like showers
jokes.punchline: Leon's cutting observation about Mike's hygiene and stress
jokes.explanation: Good insult that connects hygiene to emotional state

jokes.text: And you like to use a lot cologne. Like a lot of cologne.
jokes.setup: Discussing Mike's hygiene
jokes.punchline: The repetitive emphasis on excessive cologne use
jokes.explanation: Competent but unremarkable continuation of the hygiene joke

jokes.text: What does a red sticker mean? I'm like a target for snipers?
jokes.setup: Being given a red sticker for the event
jokes.punchline: Jumping to assassination paranoia
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from simple question to paranoid conclusion

jokes.text: It means you're wearing a red sticker. Follow me, please.
jokes.setup: Alicia's paranoid question about the sticker
jokes.punchline: Sue's completely literal, unhelpful response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Sue moment - bureaucratic literal-mindedness

jokes.text: Doyle? You're kidding me. I'd rather get advice from a fucking Ouija board.
jokes.setup: Being told Senator Doyle offered input
jokes.punchline: Comparing his advice to supernatural nonsense
jokes.explanation: Vivid comparison that shows Selina's contempt for Doyle

jokes.text: She better have been in an accident.
jokes.setup: Learning Catherine is late
jokes.punchline: Preferring her daughter be injured rather than merely irresponsible
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny insight into Selina's twisted maternal priorities

jokes.text: It's beautifully put together... like me.
jokes.setup: Praising the speech rewrite
jokes.punchline: The narcissistic self-comparison
jokes.explanation: Typical Selina narcissism but not particularly clever

jokes.text: I thought you just said it was locked. / Yeah, it is. It's structurally very sound. We just need to nail the wording, okay?
jokes.setup: Dan saying the speech is locked and done
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicting himself with more changes needed
jokes.explanation: Good political double-talk moment showing Dan's contradictory nature

jokes.text: This 'Saturday Night Live' shitstorm has just hit nine on the 'Sphincter Scale.'
jokes.setup: Reporting on the SNL sketch crisis
jokes.punchline: The absurd 'Sphincter Scale' metric
jokes.explanation: Inventive and crude metric that's perfectly in tone for the show

jokes.text: So what? I had a horse as a kid. Who didn't? / Mmm... / I mean, have a pet is what I meant.
jokes.setup: Defending having a pony
jokes.punchline: Awkward backtrack when realizing how privileged it sounds
jokes.explanation: Good moment showing Selina's privilege and attempt to seem relatable

jokes.text: It's like 'SNL' is going back in time and abusing a child.
jokes.setup: Watching the SNL sketch about young Selina
jokes.punchline: Framing comedy sketch as child abuse
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny overreaction that captures the team's persecution complex

jokes.text: Like I always say, nothing less funny than a comedian.
jokes.setup: Watching SNL mock her
jokes.punchline: The ironic statement on a comedy show
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about comedy itself from a comedy character

jokes.text: If somebody takes a shit in your car, what are you gonna do? You gonna drop your trout and take a crap through the sunroof?
jokes.setup: Dan suggesting she go on SNL to own the joke
jokes.punchline: Elaborate scatological metaphor about not fighting back
jokes.explanation: Vivid and crude metaphor that's surprisingly logical

jokes.text: 'Sue' casa es su casa, sir.
jokes.setup: Kent asking to look at her diagram
jokes.punchline: Pun on her name and the Spanish phrase
jokes.explanation: Decent pun that's surprising coming from usually serious Sue

jokes.text: If you don't like the reality, go live in Oregon and make quilts with my mother. She could use the help.
jokes.setup: Dan complaining about speech rewrites
jokes.punchline: Kent's oddly specific dismissal involving his mother
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Kent moment - completely unexpected personal detail delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Some sort of optimistic warmonger with a soft spot for educated gays.
jokes.setup: Worrying about what she'll be left as after speech changes
jokes.punchline: The oddly specific and contradictory political archetype
jokes.explanation: Brilliant political satire - perfectly captures meaningless political positioning

jokes.text: Jonad! Jonad!
jokes.setup: Mike insulting Jonah
jokes.punchline: Name combining Jonah with 'gonad'
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective name-calling that becomes a running gag.

jokes.text: President Lincoln should be back from the theater by now.
jokes.setup: Waiting for something that's taking too long
jokes.punchline: Reference to Lincoln's assassination at the theater
jokes.explanation: Dark historical reference that's both clever and morbid

jokes.text: Quit your yakking, 'cause guess who's packin'!
jokes.setup: Jonah arriving at the press area
jokes.punchline: Terrible rhyming boast about having press credentials
jokes.explanation: Terrible line that's funny because it shows Jonah's awful attempts at being cool

jokes.text: Yeah, not some pissant who narrowcasts libelous poison from his mother's phone.
jokes.setup: Excluding Jonah from legitimate press
jokes.punchline: Specific insult about his unprofessional setup
jokes.explanation: Good specific insult that captures Jonah's amateurish operation

jokes.text: I'm going to have your credentials checked, Jonad.
jokes.setup: Threatening Jonah's press access
jokes.punchline: Combining Jonah and 'gonad' into an insult
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective name-calling that becomes recurring

jokes.text: Put your boss on the phone, or I will sauté your sack, you 'SNL' shitstick.
jokes.setup: Dan calling SNL to complain
jokes.punchline: Absurdly violent culinary threat
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and absurd threat that's both violent and oddly refined

jokes.text: This is like Black Friday at Wal-Mart.
jokes.setup: Crowd of people arriving
jokes.punchline: Comparing political event to chaotic shopping
jokes.explanation: Generic comparison that works but isn't particularly clever

jokes.text: Her? Just beat cancer. / Yeah, but not a bad one. An easy one, like finger or skin. Not one of the hard ones.
jokes.setup: Pointing out cancer survivor in the crowd
jokes.punchline: Ranking cancers by difficulty like they're video game levels
jokes.explanation: Shockingly dark humor that treats cancer survival like a competition

jokes.text: 'Ooh, I just made an omelet, I'm a hero!'
jokes.setup: Mocking the 'Heroic Restaurateur'
jokes.punchline: Reducing restaurant work to making breakfast
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of overwrought titles for ordinary people

jokes.text: The St. Joseph's Children's Choir has come down with a vomiting bug. All 30 of them.
jokes.setup: Explaining where the chaperones are
jokes.punchline: Entire children's choir simultaneously sick
jokes.explanation: Absurd logistical disaster that feels realistic for their operation

jokes.text: Amy was born to be with kids. / Well, peeing is fun!
jokes.setup: Amy reluctantly taking child to bathroom
jokes.punchline: Her awkward attempt to make bathroom trips sound exciting
jokes.explanation: Shows Amy's complete inability to relate to children despite Mike's claim

jokes.text: You look like a pretty toy soldier.
jokes.setup: Seeing someone in formal military-style outfit
jokes.punchline: Reducing them to a decorative object
jokes.explanation: Visual gag about someone's appearance that infantilizes them

jokes.text: I loved you on 'Saturday Night Live.' You were hysterical. / That wasn't me, though. But wasn't it funny? So funny!
jokes.setup: Mistaken identity about SNL appearance
jokes.punchline: Selina pretending to enjoy being mocked
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment of forced enthusiasm about being ridiculed

jokes.text: You can repeat 100% any time you like, ma'am. / I will, ma'am... 100%!
jokes.setup: Selina using a round number for emphasis
jokes.punchline: Gary encouraging her to repeat meaningless statistics
jokes.explanation: Shows Gary's sycophantic nature and love of meaningless precision

jokes.text: You can't have hospitals after war. That's what I'm saying. 'Cause then they sound like they're the result of war. It's the curse of the unintended narrative.
jokes.setup: Arranging speech topics in order
jokes.punchline: Overthinking how sequence creates meaning
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dan moment - overthinking everything into meaninglessness

jokes.text: Do I look like I rode in on the last 'fuck truck,' Selina?
jokes.setup: Selina treating him like he's naive
jokes.punchline: Crude variation on 'fell off the turnip truck'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant crude variation on a classic phrase that's perfectly in character

jokes.text: We can turn water into blowjobs.
jokes.setup: Mocking impossible campaign promises
jokes.punchline: Absurdly crude take on biblical miracles
jokes.explanation: Shockingly crude and brilliant escalation that perfectly captures political cynicism

jokes.text: You want me to be some sort of a party puppet? You can stick your hand up my ass and work my mouth?
jokes.setup: Doyle demanding she follow party line
jokes.punchline: Graphic puppet metaphor for political control
jokes.explanation: Vivid and crude metaphor for political manipulation

jokes.text: No, the Dee don't stand for anything. That's like me askin' you what does the 'Mike' stand for? It stands for 'Mike kinda guy.'
jokes.setup: Someone asking what Dee stands for
jokes.punchline: Her logical but absurd explanation using Mike as example
jokes.explanation: Great Dee moment with circular logic that's somehow convincing

jokes.text: My name is Dee. And you're what, fucking nobody? 'Cause don't nobody talk to Dee like that, okay?
jokes.setup: Leon challenging her
jokes.punchline: Dee's aggressive self-advocacy with third-person reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dee moment - fierce and using her name as a title

jokes.text: They're gonna what? / Is this your doing? Did you give them this idea, Dan? / I don't do offended, but I am... affronted that you'd even think that.
jokes.setup: Learning SNL will do another sketch
jokes.punchline: Dan's pretentious distinction between offended and affronted
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dan moment - making meaningless distinctions between similar words

jokes.text: I'll tell you what happens. They get bullied when they're little at school, and then they perpetuate the cycle by bullying me.
jokes.setup: Complaining about comedians mocking her
jokes.punchline: Psychoanalyzing comedy as revenge for childhood trauma
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-pitying analysis that makes her the victim of all comedy

jokes.text: Great minds think alike! / You look like you're in one of those body swap comedies.
jokes.setup: Catherine and Selina wearing similar outfits
jokes.punchline: Ben's cutting observation about their matching clothes
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag about their unfortunate costume coordination

jokes.text: What in the wide world of fuck do you think you're wearing?
jokes.setup: Seeing Catherine's outfit choice
jokes.punchline: Elaborate profane version of 'what in the world'
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of a common phrase with perfectly placed profanity

jokes.text: This makes me look like I'm some sort of a crazy person that was deliberately trying to make us look like twins.
jokes.setup: Complaining about matching outfits
jokes.punchline: Over-analyzing the clothing choice as deliberate psychological warfare
jokes.explanation: Shows Selina's paranoid interpretation of innocent coincidence

jokes.text: I just got off the phone with the congressional leadership and they're stuck in traffic, so we may not make it to the steps.
jokes.setup: Political threat about skipping the announcement
jokes.punchline: Presenting obvious lie as traffic report
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben moment - delivering threats as mundane logistics

jokes.text: So I'm supposed to let a bunch of dead-eyed white guys... shit all over absolutely everything that I stand for.
jokes.setup: Responding to political pressure
jokes.punchline: Vivid description of congressional leadership
jokes.explanation: Accurate and cutting description of political establishment

jokes.text: I've decided that I'm... going to let them dictate to me. Because that is my decision. Do you understand that? I am letting them do that!
jokes.setup: Having to cave to political pressure
jokes.punchline: Trying to frame submission as personal choice
jokes.explanation: Brilliant political psychology - reframing defeat as empowerment

jokes.text: What's wrong with that lady's face? Looks like something crawled up in there and died.
jokes.setup: Seeing someone's unpleasant expression
jokes.punchline: Vivid description of a sour face
jokes.explanation: Classic Dee insult with vivid imagery

jokes.text: What's wrong? You look like you killed a hooker.
jokes.setup: Amy's guilty expression
jokes.punchline: Extremely dark comparison for looking guilty
jokes.explanation: Shocking escalation from normal person about guilty expression

jokes.text: This is unbelievable. / I know, and I apologize if we have wasted your time. / No, you ain't wasted nobody's time. We gonna use that time to kick your ass.
jokes.setup: Mike apologizing for wasting their time
jokes.punchline: Dee's creative reframing of time as energy for revenge
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dee logic - converting wasted time into ass-kicking energy

jokes.text: I'm angry too, I wanna kick my own ass. I'll do it for you. I've punched myself in the face before. I ran into a wall once.
jokes.setup: Trying to defuse Dee's anger
jokes.punchline: Escalating offer of self-harm ending in accidental injury
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mike moment - escalating desperation ending in pathetic admission

jokes.text: Ow! I'm trying to help you, you stupid cow!
jokes.setup: Physical altercation with Dee
jokes.punchline: The insult that creates the scandal
jokes.explanation: The catalyst moment that drives the episode's crisis

jokes.text: Where I grew up, my dad called my mom a cow all the time. It's not bad, it's homey and friendly. 'I love you, baby, you're a cow.'
jokes.setup: Trying to explain away the insult
jokes.punchline: Making it worse with fake family backstory
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cringe moment of Mike making everything worse

jokes.text: Oh, my God, Mike. What are you going to do for an encore? You gonna punch the kid?
jokes.setup: Mike's disastrous handling of the situation
jokes.punchline: Predicting even worse behavior
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Mike's potential for disaster

jokes.text: Out that door before I nail your squawky throat to it.
jokes.setup: Ordering people to leave
jokes.punchline: Violent but weirdly specific threat
jokes.explanation: Great Dee threat with creative construction imagery

jokes.text: I see you found it... the 'Room of Regret.'
jokes.setup: Finding Selina hiding in a storage room
jokes.punchline: Naming it like it's an official location
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben moment - naming emotional states like destinations

jokes.text: It's an ancient technique that's been plied by loveable losers since way back. It's called begging.
jokes.setup: Offering solution to Selina's problem
jokes.punchline: Formal description of desperate pleading
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Ben moment treating humiliation as ancient wisdom

jokes.text: You gotta go lower than the lowest lowlife. You gotta dig and dig and dig until you get to the point where you wish you were dead. Okay? And that's base camp.
jokes.setup: Explaining how to properly beg
jokes.punchline: Treating rock bottom as just the starting point
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant escalation using mountain climbing metaphor for degradation

jokes.text: It's like my 21st birthday. Or my 18th birthday. Or every other birthday, okay?
jokes.setup: Relating to Selina's bad day
jokes.punchline: Expanding from specific disappointments to lifetime pattern
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Catherine's lifelong disappointment

jokes.text: Posing for Christmas cards, watching C-SPAN instead of 'Sesame Street,' going to memorials of old Israeli men that I've never even heard of.
jokes.setup: Describing her childhood sacrifices
jokes.punchline: Specific absurd examples of political kid problems
jokes.explanation: Brilliant specific details of growing up as a political child

jokes.text: So you need to go out there. And you need to stop behaving like a little bitch.
jokes.setup: Catherine's motivational speech
jokes.punchline: Daughter calling mother a bitch for her own benefit
jokes.explanation: Shocking role reversal with daughter being brutal to mother

jokes.text: And that jacket doesn't work, by the way. You look like a waiter.
jokes.setup: Catherine's tough love speech
jokes.punchline: Selina's petty comeback about appearance
jokes.explanation: Perfect petty response showing Selina getting the last word

jokes.text: I'm appealing to your better nature. / Mike, I don't have one of those.
jokes.setup: Mike trying to appeal to Jonah's conscience
jokes.punchline: Jonah's honest admission about lacking morals
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah moment - completely honest about his amorality

jokes.text: Spin the ol' dial on Radio McClintock, see what it lands on.
jokes.setup: Demanding Mike sing for him
jokes.punchline: Treating Mike like a human jukebox
jokes.explanation: Creative metaphor for humiliating Mike's performance

jokes.text: Sir, this is your beauty pageant of the nearly dead.
jokes.setup: Organizing elderly people for photo op
jokes.punchline: Describing seniors as death-adjacent beauty contestants
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and specific description of political pageantry

jokes.text: That was fantastic, Mike. That was fantastic. Get up. / That was really nice. / Thank you. I owe you one. / What do you owe me for? / Because you're not gonna run it. / Mike, I'm absolutely running the story.
jokes.setup: Mike thinking his humiliation earned mercy
jokes.punchline: Jonah revealing it was all for nothing
jokes.explanation: Perfect cruel twist showing Jonah's complete lack of honor

jokes.text: You forgot to say it was off the record. That's like Journalism 101.
jokes.setup: Mike's shock at being betrayed
jokes.punchline: Jonah lecturing about journalism ethics while being unethical
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony of Jonah being technically correct while morally wrong

jokes.text: You are the Rosa Parks of child care! / At least Rosa got to sit down, right?
jokes.setup: Dee comparing Alicia to civil rights icon
jokes.punchline: Alicia noting Rosa Parks had it easier
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark twist on civil rights comparison with literal interpretation

jokes.text: Jonah, you are the dick that keeps on giving!
jokes.setup: Jonah causing more problems
jokes.punchline: Variation on 'gift that keeps on giving' with crude twist
jokes.explanation: Decent variation on common phrase that fits Mike's frustration

jokes.text: I'm the cow! / You are. / I'm the cow! / Moo!
jokes.setup: Mike trying to take back the insult
jokes.punchline: Alicia making him literally moo
jokes.explanation: Good humiliation payoff with literal animal sound requirement

tmdb_overview: While Selina and her staff prepare for an Annapolis event where she will announce her candidacy, Dan gets word that "Saturday Night Live" has done a not-so-flattering sketch about her.
insight: Elite callback density drives this 90-plus hour—89 jokes in 22 minutes across character work.
